messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Question on package update and version bump
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Question on package update and version bump
messages.body_plain:
Once your package was accepted it is no longer built on demand and no longer in the github submission queue.  It is added to the daily builder. Depending if it is a software or an other type of package it can be built daily to once or twice a week. see the schedule on the build report front page:

http://bioconductor.org/checkResults/

That being said it looks like you pushed Friday. It should have been reflected in Saturday's report since you made the change before the daily pull of package.  However it looks like we had issues with builds over the weekend and saturday's report did not post.  You can check the date of the report at the top of the build page (for either release or devel)

http://bioconductor.org/checkResults/3.16/bioc-LATEST/long-report.html

Yes we recommend only pushing changes to devel branch. Release is meant to be stable and only include a bug fix or bug correction.  New features or updates should only be applied to devel.  If your change was a bug correction you will need to push to the RELEASE_3_15 branch.  See the git help page for this if appropriate:


http://contributions.bioconductor.org/git-version-control.html#bug-fix-in-release-and-devel





Lori Shepherd - Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

messages.body_plain:
Hi,

They will be updated when the builds finish today. (The report is updated but we still need to propagate the new windows and mac binaries.) Devel builders run everyday except Sunday and there's a new build report for release Monday, Wednesday, and Friday.

I updated R on the devel builders on Friday so some of them didn't complete Saturday's run to update the build report, which sometimes happens with a fresh install of R and may have added some confusion.

Jennifer Wokaty

Bioconductor Core Team
CUNY SPH

messages.body_plain:
Have you installed gfortran for Apple silicon Macs as described at https://mac.r-project.org/tools/ ? I have an older version installed

/opt/R/arm64/gfortran/lib/gcc/aarch64-apple-darwin20.2.0/11.0.0/libemutls_w.a

messages.body_snippet:
Have you installed gfortran for Apple silicon Macs as described at https://mac.r-project.org/tools/ ? I have an older version installed

$ find /opt/R -name "libemutls*"
/opt/R/arm64/gfortran/lib/gcc/

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Issues with compiling edgeR 3.38.1 on macOS ARM
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Issues with compiling edgeR 3.38.1 on macOS ARM
messages.body_plain:
Hi Martin,

Awesome, thanks for the info!!! I had an older version installed. I had
installed what I thought were all the libraries, but gfortran was missing.

sudo R
source("https://mac.R-project.org/bin/install.R")
install.libs("all")

That's what I had used from
https://twitter.com/lcolladotor/status/1519665235652579328.


Here's the older version of gfortran I had (note the SDK soft link was
broken):


% ls -lh /opt/R/arm64/gfortran
total 0
lrwxr-xr-x  1 leocollado wheel   98 Dec 26  2020 SDK ->
/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Platforms/MacOSX.platform/Developer/SDKs/MacOSX11.0.sdk
drwxr-xr-x 22 leocollado staff  704 Jan  1  2021 bin
drwxr-xr-x  3 leocollado staff   96 Dec 26  2020 include
drwxr-xr-x 41 leocollado staff 1.3K Jan  1  2021 lib
drwxr-xr-x  3 leocollado staff   96 Dec 26  2020 libexec
drwxr-xr-x  5 leocollado staff  160 Dec 26  2020 share
Rdeps %
% ls -lh
/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Platforms/MacOSX.platform/Developer/SDKs/MacOSX11.0.sdk
gls: cannot access
'/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Platforms/MacOSX.platform/Developer/SDKs/MacOSX11.0.sdk':
No such file or directory

Here's the new version of gfortran (SDK soft link works):

% sudo tar fxz gfortran-12.0.1-20220312-is-darwin20-arm64.tar.xz -C /
% ls -lh  /opt/R/arm64/gfortran/
total 0
lrwxr-xr-x  1 root admin   53 Mar 21 00:46 SDK ->
/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/SDKs/MacOSX11.sdk
drwxr-xr-x 32 root admin 1.0K Jun  7 16:45 bin
drwxr-xr-x  3 root admin   96 Mar 16 18:55 include
drwxr-xr-x 47 root admin 1.5K Jun  7 16:45 lib
drwxr-xr-x  3 root admin   96 Mar 16 18:55 libexec
drwxr-xr-x  7 root admin  224 Jun  7 16:45 share
% ls -lh /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/SDKs/MacOSX11.sdk
lrwxr-xr-x 1 root wheel 14 May 24 17:47
/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/SDKs/MacOSX11.sdk -> MacOSX11.3.sdk
% find /opt/R -name "libemutls*"
/opt/R/arm64/gfortran/lib/gcc/aarch64-apple-darwin20.2.0/11.0.0/libemutls_w.a
/opt/R/arm64/gfortran/lib/gcc/aarch64-apple-darwin20.6.0/12.0.1/libemutls_w.a

I also added this to my ~/.zshrc file:

export PATH=$PATH:/opt/R/arm64/gfortran/bin


Thank you again!
Leo

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] monocle package on Bioconductor
messages.body_plain:
Hi,

As the error message says, there is a version number issue. You can only bump the ?patch? version i.e x.y.z -> x.y.z+1.

You cannot bump the ?minor? version number. 

> http://bioconductor.org/developers/how-to/version-numbering/

Please send these questions to bioc-deve at r-project.org, where there is a community to help you.


Best,

Nitesh Turaga
Scientist II, Department of Data Science,
Bioconductor Core Team Member
Dana Farber Cancer Institute

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] EventPointer Bioconductor package
messages.body_plain:
Hi,

To trigger a build you must bump the version of the package, i.e ?x.y.z? -> ?x.y.z+1?.

In your case for devel, it should be bumped to ?3.5.1? to trigger a new build after pushing up a commit. Same in release, ?3.4.1?

https://contributions.bioconductor.org/versionnum.html

Best,

Nitesh 

Nitesh Turaga
Scientist II, Department of Data Science,
Bioconductor Core Team Member
Dana Farber Cancer Institute

To trigger a build you must bump the version of the package, i.e ?x.y.z? -> ?x.y.z+1?.

In your case for devel, it should be bumped to ?3.5.1? to trigger a new build after pushing up a commit. Sa

Jim

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Convert personal count to organization
messages.body_plain:
Hi, Samuel.

If you are using the same GitHub login (your personal username, same SSH key, in other words), and you most likely are, there probably aren?t any Bioconductor changes to be made. For you, change the URL and perhaps the BugReports tags in the package DESCRIPTION file. And of course, for your local working copy, you?ll need to change the GitHub remote from your personal repo to the organization repo. The Bioconductor remote can remain the same.

Hope that helps.

Sean

Hello all,

We will be doing some updates and testing on the new package submission code today.  There may be periods of downtime where the build on demand with version bump is unavailable. We appreciate your patience and hope to have the updates and testing completed in a timely fashion.
This only affects packages that are under review and not the daily builders.

Cheers,
